items.title: Ostium suffers $18 million exploit as oracle attack wave continues to hit DeFi
items.description: A hacker used Ostium's own price-reporting infrastructure against the protocol, submitting falsified future-dated oracle data to manufacture fake trading profits and trigger an $18 million payout.

items.title: The privacy paradox of protecting kids online
items.description: We don’t need to imagine the privacy pitfalls of age verification. They’ve been happening for years, argues Cardano Foundation CEO Frederik Gregaard.

items.title: Crypto Long & Short: To ETH or not to ETH — is SOL the better diversifier?
items.description: In this week's, Morgan Stanley’s Denny Galindo writes that as the crypto market expands, solana has historically been a better portfolio diversifier than ether, despite being more volatile.

items.title: President Trump expected to meet with senators to work on ethics concerns in crypto bill
items.description: The most contentious piece of the crypto market structure bill is unresolved in the final weeks of Senate runway, and the president is expected to discuss it.

items.title: South Korea to modify 76-year-old law to classify cryptocurrencies as national assets
items.description: The government also reaffirmed plans to pilot tokenized government bonds next year and explore tokenizing state-owned real estate.

items.title: Open USD poses biggest threat yet to Circle's USDC, CoinShares says
items.description: The consortium-backed stablecoin would share reserve income with partners instead of the issuer, pressuring Circle's margins if it debuts in 2026.

items.title: A timeline of the Ethereum Foundation's ongoing shakeup
items.description: In this week's edition of The Protocol Newsletter, we’re unpacking the timeline of all the changes at the Ethereum Foundation this year.

items.title: BlackRock's crypto assets fall 39% despite $15 billion of net inflows
items.description: BlackRock's digital asset funds attracted $15 billion in net inflows over the past year, but falling crypto prices drove the value of those holdings sharply lower.

items.title: Aave launches V4 on Avalanche, laying groundwork for tokenized credit markets
items.description: The deployment marks Aave V4’s first expansion beyond Ethereum and introduces lending infrastructure designed to support future markets for tokenized real-world assets.

items.title: Securitize, Cantor target tokenized IPOs for public markets
items.description: Securitize and Cantor Fitzgerald are developing infrastructure for tokenized IPOs and secondary equity offerings within the existing US securities framework.

items.title: Bitcoin hits $65.5K as more surprise US inflation data sparks three-week BTC price high
items.description: Bitcoin moved up to its highest levels since June 22 as US PPI inflation numbers provided the week’s second surprise macro data drop.

items.title: Is Robinhood Chain’s success bullish or bearish for ETH the asset?
items.description: Surging volumes on Robinhood Chain could be very good for Ethereum, but only if the “ETH is money” crowd turn out to be right.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Crypto firms face AML risks during post-MiCA migration, says AMLA chair
items.description: AMLA is expanding its crypto oversight as firms complete the transition to the EU’s MiCA licensing regime.

items.title: Japan passes crypto overhaul to bring digital assets under financial rules
items.description: Japan’s revised Financial Instruments and Exchange Act introduces crypto insider trading rules, tougher penalties and new oversight requirements for crypto businesses.

items.title: South Korea to bring digital assets under new state asset management system
items.description: South Korea’s Economy Ministry plans to include digital assets and intellectual property under the country’s new state-asset management framework.

items.title: Warren Buffett on the market today: 'It's tough to find values when everybody is preferring gambling'
items.description: The investor has been sharply critical of the stock market, one that he says is increasingly defined by speculative trading as opposed to long-term investing.

items.title: SpaceX stock sinks below $135 IPO price for the first time
items.description: SpaceX shares fell for a fourth-straight session on Wednesday days after entering the Nasdaq-100

items.title: Trump's DNI pick Jay Clayton won't tell senators Biden won the 2020 election
items.description: The Senate Intelligence Committee is considering Jay Clayton's nomination to be the director of national of intelligence.

items.title: New York Fed President Williams says inflation has peaked, rates 'well positioned'
items.description: Williams cited five reasons why he expects the latest price surge has run its course.

items.title: Wholesale prices unexpectedly declined 0.3% in June on big drop in gasoline
items.description: As with consumer prices, the index benefited from easing energy costs, particularly as oil fell due to the brief pause in tensions between the U.S. and Iran.

items.title: Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh's testimony to Senate banking committee hits on economy, interest rates
items.description: Part of congressionally mandated Capitol Hill appearances for the central bank leader, Warsh spoke Tuesday to the House Financial Services Committee.

items.title: Todd Blanche says he has 'full faith' in Kash Patel as FBI director
items.description: Attorney General nominee Todd Blanche previously served as a criminal defense lawyer for President Donald Trump.

items.title: Mortgage rates rise to highest level in nearly a year, causing homebuyers to pause
items.description: Mortgage rates moved higher last week, causing buyers to pull back, but refinancing did see small gains.

items.description: Gamble on resuming hostilities raises prospect that conflict will overshadow president’s second term

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=thr6MYyGr0s! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: It’s a buyer’s market for bonds
items.description: Bonds are out of fashion with big investors — which makes them an interesting investment opportunity for the rest of us.

items.title: America’s oil refiners could more than triple profits as Iran war sparks massive boom
items.description: U.S. oil refiners are about to report a historic profit surge as the industry takes advantage of supply disruptions.

items.title: More people are reaching their 60s than ever before — and it’s an incredible investing opportunity
items.description: The world is getting older. Here’s how that’s going to change things.

items.title: This overlooked index is a better investment than the S&P 500
items.description: We did the math over a 30-year period, and it’s not even close.

items.title: Copper is shadowing the AI hyperscaler stocks — but wait for a dip before buying
items.description: Copper prices have climbed 12.5% since the start of the year, with Nvidia and Broadcom up around the same amount in that time.

items.title: BlackRock has best-ever start to a year as investment flows double and profits surge
items.description: Shares of BlackRock were rallying toward their best day in more than a year on Wednesday, after strong inflows into exchange-traded funds and buoyant markets helped the world’s biggest asset manager beat Wall Street’s earnings expectations for the latest quarter.

items.title: You are missing the bond deal of the decade — and it is guaranteed to beat inflation
items.description: TIPS are offering a rare gift — and it is time to lock in a generous payout.

items.title: Buying a house is getting easier by one measure. Here’s why it doesn’t feel like it.
items.description: Home prices are growing much slower than they did during the pandemic-era buying frenzy.

items.title: Is shock the new reality? | FT Rethink
items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OTGqVSmG3cw! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. items.title: Shabana Mahmood set to be named UK chancellor by Andy Burnham
items.description: Current home secretary expected to succeed Rachel Reeves as finance minister

items.title: VAT is Burnham’s best tax-raising tool if UK public finances go off track, says OECD
items.description: New report argues long-term reforms should focus on spending restraint, including end to pensions triple lock

items.title: The UK finally has a blueprint for innovation — now it must act on it
items.description: The Whittle Lab aims to transform aerothermal engineering, one of the country’s strongest manufacturing industries

items.title: The UK is forgetting the dull bit of climate change policy
items.description: Adaptation has never been the priority, but this summer’s heat is a message that the Treasury needs to act

items.title: Buffett describes Gates’s relationship with Epstein as ‘distasteful’
items.description: Billionaire Berkshire chair says he could ‘picture’ himself making a similar mistake in choosing associates

items.title: Oil traders warn market is close to running on empty as Hormuz shuts again
items.description: Stockpiles that acted as shock absorbers early in Iran war are running low as key waterway closes once more

items.title: Morgan Stanley wealth management boosted by SpaceX millionaires
items.description: Investment bank becomes latest to shatter forecasts on back of AI boom

items.title: Thames Water warns over long-term future
items.description: Utility says it can survive 12 months but cautions that liquidity may fall short before recapitalisation

items.title: Goldman’s Former Top Lawyer Calls Epstein a ‘Masterful Liar’
items.description: Kathryn Ruemmler, the former general counsel of Goldman Sachs, testified on Wednesday in a closed-door congressional hearing investigating Jeffery Epstein.

items.title: IBM’s Stock Crash Reignites Fears of A.I. Disruption in Software Sector
items.description: The company tumbled sharply after previewing quarterly results that were disappointing. It’s not good news for software and tech consulting companies.

items.title: Oil Prices Tick Higher as U.S. Reinstates Iran Blockade
items.description: The United States reinstated its blockade on Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz and shipping slowed to a crawl amid the renewed warfare in the critical waterway.

items.title: A Year After DOGE Cuts, Social Security Is Trying to Stabilize
items.description: The agency has been quietly hiring workers again, and its leadership is focused on improving technology to ease workloads left by the departure of about 7,800 staff members.

items.title: The American E.V. Has Been Crushed. Will It Take the U.S. Auto Industry With It?
items.description: The largest U.S. automakers have backed away from electric vehicles, even as global sales are booming. The decision may make them obsolete.

items.title: From Twitter to X: 20 Years of Memes, Movements and Hot Takes
items.description: The first tweets from the public appeared on July 15, 2006, several months after a soft launch. It was rebranded as X after being acquired by Elon Musk.

items.title: What Trump’s New Iran Blockade Could Mean for Oil Prices
items.description: The first blockade in April choked off Iranian exports without causing a lasting price surge. Now global oil reserves are lower, and ships face heightened risks.

items.title: China’s Chip Champion to Raise Billions in Race for A.I. Control
items.description: CXMT, a linchpin in China’s drive to develop homegrown artificial intelligence technology, is aiming to raise nearly $10 billion in a blockbuster public offering in Shanghai.
